title: betula verrucosa var. odorata
intro: Betula verrucosa var. odorata, also known as the Birch Betula or Sweet Birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive papery white bark. The bark of Betula verrucosa var. odorata contains salicin, a compound with medicinal properties similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. The health benefits of Betula verrucosa var. odorata include its ability to alleviate headaches, muscle aches, and joint pain, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain cardiovascular diseases. Herbal preparations of Betula verrucosa var. odorata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the bark in hot water or a solvent, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
